promotion_target: promote beam from hint-guided preview-derived 3d candidate to verified preview

ranked_experiments.goal: Recover stable record-local NODE xyz semantics for the 951..1111 range.
ranked_experiments.why_first: Without record-local xyz, every later connectivity claim stays underdetermined.
method: Scan the NODE hinted range as 32-byte records and score candidate slot/record assemblers beyond the current packed-pair hint path.
Test whether xyz are split across adjacent records or mixed float/uint slots rather than living in one record.
Promote only if a repeatable assembler yields many 3-value node tuples with building-scale axis spans.
expected_evidence: NODE decode artifact with materially more resolved xyz tuples than the current 1 record with 2 plausible values.
A clear slot/record mapping rule that is stable across the whole NODE range.
Candidate node cloud size large enough to exceed the current 5-point hint preview by an order-of-magnitude margin.
success_criteria: Recovered node tuples are consistent across most of the 160 hinted NODE records.
A parser-side node decode note can say exact xyz recovery is justified, not merely hinted.
failure_signals: Recovered tuples only exist in isolated rows.
Axis spans collapse or vary wildly between assemblers.
The decode still depends on one-off hand-picked records instead of a repeatable rule.
ranked_experiments.unlocks: Meaningful candidate node coordinates for exact topology reconstruction.

ranked_experiments.goal: Recover trustworthy ELEM member references for the 224..379 range and connect them to the recovered NODE ids.
ranked_experiments.why_second: Connectivity is currently the explicit blocker in the viewer and must become trustworthy before any verified promotion.
method: Profile the ELEM range as 8x uint32 records and isolate slots whose value distribution overlaps the recovered NODE id domain.
Reject constant-filled or scaffolding-like slots and keep only slots that repeatedly resolve to valid NODE references.
Construct provisional member paths and measure how many ELEM records resolve into stable node-to-node edges.
expected_evidence: A candidate elem reference slot map with repeatable NODE id references.
Resolved member paths for a meaningful subset of the 155 ELEM records.
A topology-grounded preview whose edges come from ELEM references rather than record-order polylines.
success_criteria: Connectivity no longer looks constant-filled or scaffolding-like.
Resolved member reference rate is high enough to explain most active ELEM records.
The parser can emit a topology-grounded preview with geometry_preview_ready=true.
failure_signals: Reference slots stay dominated by constants or out-of-range ids.
Recovered connectivity is too sparse to exceed the current hint-guided preview.
ranked_experiments.unlocks: Record-grounded member connectivity strong enough to claim exact/topology-grounded recovery.

ranked_experiments.goal: Re-run decode and viewer truth after NODE xyz and ELEM connectivity recovery, then decide verified-preview promotion.
ranked_experiments.why_third: Promotion should be a gate check, not a decoding experiment.
method: Re-run the beam decoder to regenerate inventory/report artifacts.
Rebuild the preview bridge and inspect the viewer metadata.
Apply the promotion gate criteria below without relaxing them.
expected_evidence: viewer status moves above unverified hint preview.
cross-check quality improves materially from weak silhouette match.
success_criteria: All promotion gate criteria pass together.
No blocker note remains about untrustworthy ELEM connectivity.
failure_signals: Preview quality improves visually but still lacks parser-side exactness evidence.
ranked_experiments.unlocks: Safe promotion decision grounded in regenerated evidence.

promotion_gate_criteria: NODE xyz recovery must be justified by a repeatable record/slot rule, not sparse scalar hints.
ELEM connectivity must be resolved by trustworthy node-reference semantics, not record-order polyline heuristics.
Viewer blocker text about constant-filled ELEM connectivity must disappear.
Cross-check quality must improve beyond weak silhouette match.
Primary member probe / adapter readiness must move beyond table layout still uncertain.
bottom_line: Do not spend time on promotion wording before experiments 1 and 2 produce parser-side evidence. The real work is NODE xyz recovery first, ELEM connectivity second, gate check third.
